plan: Trim SPY to 20% to meet the single-name concentration mandate and reduce overall exposure.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure, as SOXL is also below its stop.
Reduce SMH to 8% to lower exposure to a downtrending sector.
Increase QQQI to 25% for more income and broad market exposure, aligning with capital preservation.
Buy XLV at 5% to add a high-quality, diversified sector with a strong scanner read.
These moves aim for capital preservation, lower leverage, and increased diversification.
conservative.read: This conservative plan targets around 1.5% per month, focusing on capital preservation and minimizing drawdown. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration, but potential upside is limited.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to comply with the single-name concentration mandate.
Sell SOXL entirely due to it being below its stop and to reduce overall leverage.
Reduce SPAL to 3% to keep leveraged ETF exposure small and within limits.
Trim SMH to 10% to reduce exposure to a downtrending sector while maintaining some chip exposure.
Increase CRWD to 15% to press into a working AI/Software position with strong P&L.
Buy LRCX at 5% to add a high-scoring semiconductor name, balancing sector exposure.
balanced.read: This balanced plan aims for approximately 2.5% per month, prioritizing risk-first growth. It reduces overall leverage and concentration while still seeking growth opportunities. Drawdowns are expected to be moderate.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the single-name concentration mandate.
Sell SOXL entirely as it is below its stop; reallocate funds to stronger setups.
Reduce SMH to 8% to decrease exposure to a downtrending sector.
Increase CRWD to 20% to aggressively press into a high-performing AI/Software position.
Buy LRCX at 10% and ASML at 7% to add high-scoring semiconductor names, concentrating in strong setups.
Increase SPAL to 8% to utilize leveraged ETFs for enhanced growth, staying within the combined limit.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets around 4% per month, aiming for stretched growth by leveraging strong setups. It involves higher concentration and increased use of leveraged ETFs, leading to significantly larger potential drawdowns and daily decay. Strict stop discipline is crucial.

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and free up capital for income-focused assets.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to completely eliminate leveraged ETF exposure.
Sell SMH to exit a high-beta, downtrending sector, further de-risking the portfolio.
Reduce CRWD to 5% to significantly lower exposure to high-beta single names.
Increase QQQI to 35% to maximize exposure to income-generating ETFs for steady distributions.
These moves prioritize low drawdown and reliable income over growth, aiming for a cash-like income profile.
income.read: This income plan targets around 1% per month, focusing on parking cash for yield with monthly distributions. It significantly reduces growth-oriented and leveraged assets, prioritizing low drawdown. Distributions are variable, and NAV can still fall in a selloff.
